info.title: FastAPI Bootstrap
info.description: Production-grade FastAPI backend with auth (JWT+OAuth+RBAC) and LLM chat proxy domains.

get.description:
Return service health status.

Used by docker-compose healthcheck, load balancers, and ``make health``.

Response body::

    {"status": "ok", "env": "development"}

Return service readiness — verifies DB, Redis, and SMTP are reachable.

Local development runs FastAPI on the host while docker-compose exposes
PostgreSQL, Redis, and Mailpit on localhost. This endpoint performs real
network checks against those configured endpoints so ``make smoke-test``
can prove the host-run app can reach the local infra stack.

Returns 200 when all dependencies respond; 503 if any are unavailable.

post.description:
Register a new user account and send a verification email.

FastAPI validates and normalizes ``payload`` before this handler delegates
registration to the auth application service.  The response does NOT
include a JWT — the user must verify their email first (or the calling
client skips verification in non-prod envs).

Raises
------
409
    If a user with the given email already exists.
Mark a user's email as verified using the token from the email link.
Authenticate with email + password and receive an access + refresh JWT pair.

Exchange a refresh token for a new access + refresh token pair.

The old refresh token is immediately revoked.  Reuse of an already-rotated
token triggers family revocation (all sessions for the user are terminated).

Raises
------
401
    If the token is invalid, expired, revoked, or a reuse attack is detected.

Revoke the refresh token and blacklist the current access token.

The client should discard both tokens after calling this endpoint.

Send a password-reset link to the given email.

Always returns the same 202 response regardless of whether the email exists
(prevents user enumeration).
Apply a password-reset token and set a new password.

get.description: Return the profile of the currently authenticated user.

Generate an OAuth2 authorization URL for the given provider.

The returned ``state`` nonce is stored in Redis for CSRF validation in the
callback.  Supported providers: google,kakao,naver.

Raises
------
400
    If *provider* is not configured or unsupported.

get.summary: OAuth2 callback — exchange code for JWT
get.description:
Complete the OAuth2 flow by exchanging the code for user tokens.

Validates the ``state`` nonce against Redis to prevent CSRF attacks.

Raises
------
400
    If the state is invalid or expired.
502
    If the OAuth provider returns an error.

post.description: Send a conversation to the active LLM provider and receive the full response in a single JSON body.  Provider is set via ``LLM_PROVIDER``.
Stream the LLM response as Server-Sent Events.  Each ``data`` event carries a text chunk.  A final ``data: [DONE]`` event signals the end.

get.description: Return the currently active LLM provider name, model, and full LiteLLM model identifier.  Useful for health checks and integration tests.
Return all conversations for the authenticated user (newest first).

schema.title: Response List Conversations Api V1 Chat Conversations Get

post.description:
Create a new conversation session for the authenticated user.

Requires the ``chat:write`` permission.

get.description: Return a conversation owned by the authenticated user.
Return all messages in a conversation (oldest first).

schema.title: Response List Messages Api V1 Chat Conversations  Conversation Id  Messages Get

post.summary: Send a message — SSE streaming + DB persistence
post.description:
Send a user message and receive an SSE-streamed assistant reply.

Workflow
--------
1. Verify conversation ownership.
2. Persist the user message.
3. Rebuild LangChain message list from conversation history.
4. Stream tokens from the LLM provider.
5. Collect all chunks; persist the completed assistant message.
6. If this is the first turn, auto-generate a title.

Returns an :class:`~sse_starlette.sse.EventSourceResponse` when
``body.stream=True`` (default); returns a :class:`MessageResponse` JSON
body when ``body.stream=False``.

Requires the ``chat:write`` permission.

schema.title: Response Send Message Api V1 Chat Conversations  Conversation Id  Messages Post

ChatMessage.title: ChatMessage
ChatMessage.description:
A single message in a conversation turn.

Attributes
----------
role:
    Speaker role — ``"user"``, ``"assistant"``, or ``"system"``.
    Unknown roles default to ``HumanMessage``.
content:
    Text content of the message.

ChatRequest.title: ChatRequest
ChatRequest.description:
Request body for chat endpoints.

Attributes
----------
messages:
    Ordered conversation history.  Must contain at least one message.
system:
    Optional system prompt prepended before all other messages.
    Equivalent to adding a ``{"role": "system", ...}`` entry at the front.

ChatResponse.title: ChatResponse
ChatResponse.description:
Response body for the non-streaming ``/complete`` endpoint.

Attributes
----------
content:
    The model's full reply text.
model:
    Active LiteLLM model identifier, e.g. ``"openai/gpt-4o-mini"``.
    Included for debugging and provider-routing verification.

PasswordResetRequest.title: PasswordResetRequest
PasswordResetRequest.description: Request body for POST /auth/password-reset (request reset link).

message.title: Message
message.default: If an account with that email exists, a reset link has been sent.

PasswordResetRequestResponse.title: PasswordResetRequestResponse
PasswordResetRequestResponse.description:
Response body for POST /auth/password-reset.

The message is intentionally generic to prevent account enumeration.

ProviderInfoResponse.title: ProviderInfoResponse
ProviderInfoResponse.description:
Response body for the ``/provider`` info endpoint.

Attributes
----------
provider:
    Active provider name, e.g. ``"openai"``, ``"ollama"``.
model:
    Base model name, e.g. ``"gpt-4o-mini"``, ``"llama3.2"``.
litellm_model:
    Full LiteLLM model identifier, e.g. ``"openai/gpt-4o-mini"``.

stream.title: Stream
stream.description: When True, response is SSE-streamed. When False, returns full JSON.

TokenResponse.title: TokenResponse
TokenResponse.description: JWT pair returned by login and refresh endpoints.

UserResponse.title: UserResponse
UserResponse.description: Public user representation — never includes hashed_password.
